title: Ideas Tracking App
summary: This app — meta! A two-layer board for capturing and advancing ideas.
description: A web app where Jason shares ideas, they become tiles, and Jay-C proposes concrete next steps. Each step can be approved with one tap. The first idea in the system is the system itself.

suggestions.text: Add ability to submit new ideas via Telegram (text me an idea, I add it to the board)
Add categories/filters (health, business, tools, family, etc.)

title: Permanent Dashboard URL
summary: Set up named Cloudflare tunnel so the dashboard URL never changes.
description: Currently the tunnel URL changes every time cloudflared restarts. A named tunnel with a Cloudflare account would give a permanent URL. Could also use a custom domain.

suggestions.text: Walk Jason through Cloudflare account signup and named tunnel setup

title: Social Memory Network for Dementia
summary: A centralized memory platform built from a dementia patient's social circle to resurface memories and maintain connection.
description: Inspired by watching Jason's father-in-law's dementia progression and the parallel with AI memory formation. Core insight: memory is networked, not individual. The patient's social circle collectively holds memories the patient is losing. The platform aggregates photos, stories, voice recordings, and music from family/friends and resurfaces them to the patient at therapeutic cadence — like Apple Photos memories but crowdsourced from everyone who loves them. Quality of life play, not clinical claims. Side effect: keeps the social circle engaged with the patient (combats isolation). Potential to exercise forgotten neural pathways through familiar social triggers.

suggestions.text: Research existing products in reminiscence/dementia care space — identify gaps
Map the caregiver/family buyer persona and willingness to pay
Explore technical architecture: how contributors submit memories, how resurfacing algorithm works
Talk to dementia caregivers about pain points and what they'd actually use

d2c_analysis.verdict: Unfavorable asymmetry as a direct-to-consumer business. Real emotional/clinical value but low commercial ceiling.
d2c_analysis.upside: Reminiscence therapy is clinically validated (reduces depression Cohen's d=-0.54, improves QoL, improves communication). Social memory aggregation from patient's actual circle is genuinely novel — no existing product does this. Enormous emotional value to families (combats helplessness). ~7M US dementia patients, ~11M unpaid caregivers.
d2c_analysis.downside: No dementia consumer app has achieved venture scale. WTP ~$25/mo median. Built-in churn (patient deteriorates over time — narrow usability window). Regulatory minefield if claiming cognitive benefit (FDA territory). MindMate raised only $2M after 500K+ users. Kodak tried with massive brand recognition — minimal traction. Caregiver buyer is financially strained, time-poor, emotionally overwhelmed — hard to acquire and retain.
d2c_analysis.competitors: Remme (AI reminiscence, early stage), LifeBio Memory (care facility focused), MindMate (brain games, $2M raised), Kodak reminiscence tool (minimal traction). 50+ research apps studied, most abandoned.
d2c_analysis.recommendation: Do not build as standalone D2C company. Graveyard of failed attempts is large and clear.

provider_rtm_analysis.verdict: Meaningfully better asymmetry. Provider-billed RTM model removes hardest D2C problems (consumer WTP, acquisition cost) and maps to Jason's Court Health playbook.
provider_rtm_analysis.mechanism: CPT 98978 covers RTM for cognitive behavioral therapy data — device supply + data transmission. Platform tracks therapy adherence (patient engagement with memory content), therapy response (mood scores, behavioral changes), and delivers structured therapeutic content as part of formal treatment plan.
provider_rtm_analysis.revenue_per_patient: ~$105-145/mo (98978 ~$55 + 98980 ~$50 + potential 98981 add-ons ~$39 each). 4-6x the D2C ceiling.
provider_rtm_analysis.2026_tailwinds: CMS expanded RTM significantly: new codes 98984/98985 allow shorter monitoring periods, lower time thresholds, more documentation flexibility. Trend is clearly toward expanding what qualifies.
provider_rtm_analysis.target_providers: Primary market: geriatric psychiatrists (~1,800 board-certified US), behavioral neurologists, memory clinics. Secondary: geriatricians, psychiatrists, NPs under supervising physicians. Volume play: primary care (delivers majority of dementia care but less motivated by specialty tools).
provider_rtm_analysis.key_risk: CMS hasn't explicitly blessed reminiscence therapy under 98978 — code was designed around CBT. Needs careful clinical framing as structured digital therapeutic intervention with measurable data. Navigable but requires work.
provider_rtm_analysis.recommendation: This is the path worth exploring. B2B to clinics is Jason's exact playbook from Court Health. Next step: validate 98978 clinical framing with a geriatric psychiatrist or billing specialist.

cbt_integration_analysis.verdict: Reminiscence therapy is already a recognized CBT component for dementia in published clinical protocols. Natural additions would clearly satisfy 98978.
cbt_integration_analysis.precedent: CBTAC protocol (published RCT) explicitly combines behavioral activation, reminiscence, cognitive restructuring, and caregiver interventions into a single CBT treatment for dementia dyads.
natural_additions: Behavioral activation tracking — daily engagement with memory content + pre/post mood scoring = therapy adherence + response data
Caregiver cognitive restructuring modules — reframing grief/catastrophic thoughts, daily stress check-ins (strong evidence base for caregiver CBT)
Active cognitive stimulation prompts alongside passive memory resurfacing ('Who is this? Tell me about this place.')
Behavioral symptom logging by caregiver (agitation, sleep, wandering) for clinician treatment adjustment
cbt_integration_analysis.positioning: Not 'a reminiscence app that also does CBT' — it's a digital CBT platform for dementia patient-caregiver dyads where social memory is the behavioral activation engine. Clinically accurate per CBTAC model.
cbt_integration_analysis.98978_fit: Scheduled daily recordings (engagement tracking, mood logs), programmed alerts (disengagement, behavioral changes), therapy adherence data, therapy response data — all flow naturally from this design.
cbt_integration_analysis.bonus: Caregiver depression/anxiety from caregiving is a billable diagnosis — platform potentially serves two patients per family unit.

suggestions.text: Research existing products in reminiscence/dementia care space — identify gaps
Map the caregiver/family buyer persona and willingness to pay
Research RTM/RPM reimbursement path (98978 cognitive behavioral therapy code)
Validate 98978 clinical framing with a geriatric psychiatrist or billing specialist
Talk to dementia caregivers about pain points and what they'd actually use
Explore technical architecture: how contributors submit memories, how resurfacing algorithm works
Explore grant funding (NIA, Alzheimer's Association) if pursuing as nonprofit/research project
